name: Cable Cross-Section Calculator
seo_title: Cable Size Calculator – mm² Cross-Section – IEC 60364
seo_description: Calculate the correct electrical cable cross-section (mm²) for your circuit by current, length and voltage. Free tool with instant, standards-based results.

range_hints.corriente_a: Circuit rated current; typically 10-32 A for residential use, up to 200 A for industrial service entrances
range_hints.longitud_m: Actual cable length from distribution board to farthest load, not straight-line distance
range_hints.caida_max_pct: Maximum 3 % for lighting circuits per IEC; 5 % for power circuits. In rural areas with weak grid, reduce to 2 %

result_context: To {corriente_a}A a {length_m}m in {tension_v}V recomienda cable of {seccion_mm2}mm². Caída of voltage calculada: {caida_pct}%. Corriente máxima admisible: {imax_a}A.
mistakes: Dimensionar solo per current without Consider caída of voltage
Not Use cross-section minimum reglamentaria (2.5mm² to enchufes)
Not Consider agrupamiento of cables (reduce capacity)
trust_note: Copper conductivity at 20 °C is taken as σ = 56 m/(Ω·mm²). For aluminum reduce to 61 %. Standard cross-sections follow the series 1.5, 2.5, 4, 6, 10, 16, 25, 35, 50, 70, 95, 120 mm² per IEC 60228. For ambient temperatures above 40 °C apply derating factors per IEC 60364-5-52.
standard: IEC 60364-5-52 for low-voltage electrical installations. Maximum voltage drop is 3 % for lighting and 5 % for power circuits relative to nominal voltage. For North America, refer to NEC Article 210.19(A) with a combined 5 % limit for feeders and branch circuits.
desc: Calculate copper cable cross-section in mm² based on current, length, and voltage drop limits per IEC 60364. Free, no sign-up, instant results.
